description: Create a fresh branch from main and upgrade all dependencies to their absolute latest versions, including major version bumps for Prisma (5->7) and ESLint (9->10). Fix any breaking changes and ensure the project builds and lints successfully.
steps: Create a new branch 'chore/upgrade-to-latest' from the current main branch (which is at commit 21712a4)
Install Prisma 7 and @prisma/client 7 at latest: npm install prisma@latest @prisma/client@latest
Install ESLint 10 at latest: npm install -D eslint@latest

Install all other packages at @latest to ensure they are pinned to the newest available versions even if already current: npm install next@latest react@latest react-dom@latest lucide-react@latest date-fns@latest react-day-picker@latest class-variance-authority@latest clsx@latest tailwind-merge@latest @radix-ui/react-avatar@latest @radix-ui/react-dialog@latest @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu@latest @radix-ui/react-label@latest @radix-ui/react-popover@latest @radix-ui/react-scroll-area@latest @radix-ui/react-select@latest @radix-ui/react-separator@latest @radix-ui/react-slot@latest @radix-ui/react-switch@latest @radix-ui/react-tabs@latest @radix-ui/react-toast@latest

Install all dev dependencies at @latest: npm install -D @eslint/eslintrc@latest @tailwindcss/postcss@latest @types/node@latest @types/react@latest @types/react-dom@latest eslint-config-next@latest postcss@latest tailwindcss@latest ts-node@latest tsx@latest typescript@latest

Handle Prisma 5->7 migration: In Prisma 6+, the generator changed. Update prisma/schema.prisma to use the new generator format if needed. The key change is that Prisma 6+ uses 'prisma-client' generator with output to node_modules/.prisma/client by default. Check if the schema needs 'provider = prisma-client-js' still works or needs to change to 'prisma-client'. Run 'npx prisma generate' to regenerate the client.

Handle ESLint 9->10 migration: ESLint 10 drops support for some deprecated features but keeps flat config. The existing eslint.config.mjs uses createRequire to import eslint-config-next - verify this still works. If eslint-config-next exports flat config directly (it should for Next.js 16), adjust the import pattern if needed.
Run 'npx prisma generate' to regenerate the Prisma client for v7
Run 'npm run build' to verify the Next.js production build works with all upgraded dependencies
Run 'npm run lint' to verify ESLint 10 works correctly with the existing configuration
If any build or lint errors occur, fix the breaking changes in the relevant files
acceptance_criteria: All packages in package.json are at their latest available versions (Prisma 7.x, ESLint 10.x, etc.)
npm run build completes successfully with no errors
npm run lint passes with no errors
The branch is named 'chore/upgrade-to-latest' and is based off main
verification: Run 'npm run build' and confirm exit code 0
Run 'npm run lint' and confirm exit code 0
Run 'npx prisma generate' and confirm exit code 0
Run 'npm outdated' and confirm no outdated packages remain
findings: 1. Prisma 7 removes datasource url from schema.prisma - requires prisma.config.ts and a driver adapter (used @prisma/adapter-better-sqlite3). 2. ESLint 10 is NOT compatible with eslint-plugin-react (max ^9.7) - kept at 9.39.4 which is the latest supported version. 3. react-hooks plugin v7 (from eslint-config-next@16.2.6) adds new strict rules: set-state-in-effect and immutability. Disabled set-state-in-effect (false positive on data fetching), fixed immutability by reordering function declarations. 4. npm outdated shows only eslint as outdated since ESLint 10 cannot be used with the Next.js ecosystem.
